blurb: A tiny stacked-leaf Crassula, still just a plug — possibly an offset of one of the other Crassulas on the shelf. Filed here until it grows up enough to name confidently.

care.light: Bright, indirect light while it's small and establishing; ease it toward stronger light as it bulks up.
care.water: Soak-and-dry, but go gently — a tiny root system in a small pot dries fast yet rots easily if kept wet. Let it dry fully between drinks.
care.dormancy: Like its kin, a cool-season grower that rests in summer heat.
care.feeding: Barely, if at all, while this small.
care.potting: Leave it to settle and root before potting on; move up only a size at a time.
care.propagation: Crassulas root readily from stem and leaf cuttings — this may itself be a stray that rooted.
care.bloom: Not likely until it's much larger.
care.troubles: Its size makes it unforgiving of overwatering; keep an eye out for damping-off and mealybugs.
care.toxicity: Considered mildly toxic to cats and dogs if eaten.
